Hamza Khalid
@Whizz_ai
How to Run OpenClaw Locally on your Computer (Step by Step Guide)
This is one of the craziest innovations in the History of AI.
And people are buying Mac Minis just to run it.
Five weeks ago, an open-source project made it possible for anyone with $5 and an internet connection to run a full-time AI assistant.
Not a chatbot.
Not a search tool.
An assistant that runs on hardware you control, answers your WhatsApp messages while you sleep, and builds applications overnight on your behalf.
This guide will take you from "what is this lobster thing" to a fully operational personal AI that controls your email, calendar, smart home, and anything else you can describe in a sentence.

Why Everyone Is Losing Their Minds Over This

Let's be direct about what Clawdbot actually does differently:
Traditional AI (ChatGPT, Claude)
• You open a website • You have to start the conversation • It forgets past chats • Only answers when asked • Mostly gives information • Runs on their servers
Clawdbot
• Lives inside WhatsApp, Telegram, or Discord • Remembers conversations from weeks ago • Messages you proactively • Takes action on your behalf • Actually does things, not just answers • Can run on your own setup

The Architecture of Clawdbot

Architecture of Clawdbot
Gateway = The control plane. Connects to messaging apps, handles scheduling, runs 24/7.
Agent = The brain. Claude, GPT-4, MiniMax, or local models.
Skills = Markdown files that teach the agent how to use tools. Want it to control your Sonos? Add a skill. Want it to query your finances? Add a skill. Want it to write skills for itself? It can do that too.
Memory = Plain text files (Markdown) stored locally. Syncs with Obsidian if you want. Search with Raycast. Back up however you like.

Hardware: What You Actually Need

Stop buying Mac Minis just for this. Even the creator says you don't need one.
Minimum Requirements (Yes, Really)
  • 1 CPU core
  • 1GB RAM
  • 500MB disk space
What People Are Actually Running

Installation: Three Paths

Path 1: One-Liner (Most Users)

curl -fsSL
https://clawd.bot/install.sh
| bash
Then run the wizard:
clawdbot onboard --install-daemon
The wizard handles:
  • Authentication (API key or your existing Claude subscription)
  • Channel setup (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, etc.)
  • Background service installation
  • Workspace configuration

Path 2: VPS Deployment (Remote Access)

For Hetzner/DigitalOcean/Linode:

Create a Ubuntu 24.04 droplet (2GB RAM minimum)
SSH in as root

adduser clawd
usermod -aG sudo clawd
su - clawd

curl -fsSL
https://clawd.bot/install.sh
| bash
exec bash
clawdbot onboard --install-daemon

If npm install fails (out of memory):

Option 1: Increase Node memory
export NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=2048"

Option 2: Add swap
sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile
sudo chmod 600 /swapfile
sudo mkswap /swapfile
sudo swapon /swapfile
echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab

Secure remote access:

SSH tunnel (recommended)
ssh -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 clawd@YOUR_IP

Then open locally
open http://127.0.0.1:18789

Path 3: Docker

docker pull clawdbot/clawdbot
docker run -d clawdbot/clawdbot

Or with Docker Compose:

wget
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/clawdbot/clawdbot/main/docker-compose.yml
docker compose up -d

Authentication Options

You have three choices. Pick based on your situation.
Option A: Anthropic API Key (Recommended for Control)
Best for: Cost tracking, multiple models, production use
https://console.anthropic.com/
Model tip from the docs:
"I strongly recommend Anthropic Pro/Max + Opus 4.5 for long‑context strength and better prompt‑injection resistance."
Option B: Claude Pro/Max OAuth
Best for: Using your existing $20-200/month Claude subscription
The SDK uses your Claude Code login automatically. If you've run claude before and authenticated, you're good.
Option C: Multi-Model Setup (Power Users)
{
"models": {
"default": "minimax-m2.1",
"escalation": "claude-opus-4.5"
}
}
MiniMax handles general chat (fast, cheap). Claude Opus activates for code and complex reasoning. This is how the power users run it.

Channel Setup (Pick Your Interface)

1. Telegram (Easiest, Most Popular)
  1. Message @ BotFather on Telegram
  2. Send /newbot
  3. Name your bot
  4. Copy the token
2. WhatsApp (Power User Choice)
⚠️ Critical warning: WhatsApp has no "bot" concept. Every inbound message becomes agent input. Use a dedicated phone number.
Scan with WhatsApp → Settings → Linked Devices
Security config:
{
"channels": {
"whatsapp": {
"allowFrom": ["+15555550123"],
"groups": {
"*": { "requireMention": true }
}
}
}
}
Discord
  1. Create application at
    discord.com/developers
  2. Enable Message Content Intent (Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents)
  3. Add token to config

The Skills System: This Is Where It Gets Insane

Skills are Markdown files that teach Clawdbot what it can do. This is the core of why Clawdbot is different.
Built-in Skills
You can literally say:
Create a skill that monitors my email for flight confirmations, extracts the flight details, and adds them to my calendar. Check email every 30 minutes.
Clawdbot will:
  1. Scaffold the skill structure
  2. Write the automation logic
  3. Set up the cron job
  4. Test it
  5. Deploy it

REAL USECASE WITH DEMOS

1. The Car Negotiator
AJ Stuyvenberg used Clawdbot to buy a Hyundai Palisade:
Search
reddit.com/r/hyundaipalisade
and find the typical and lowest prices people paid for a 2026 Palisade Hybrid in Massachusetts
Use
hexorcism.com
to search dealers for a Palisade Hybrid in Calligraphy trim with green/blue exterior and brown interior. Stay within 50 miles of Boston. Find each car using the VIN on dealer sites and contact them asking for best out-the-door price.
Clawdbot:
  • Browsed multiple dealer websites
  • Filled out contact forms automatically
  • Sent pricing requests
  • Monitored email responses
  • Tracked the best offers in a spreadsheet
"Outsourcing the painful aspects of a car purchase to AI was refreshingly nice, and sold me on the vision of Clawdbot."
Link:
https://aaronstuyvenberg.com/posts/clawd-bought-a-car
2. The 14-Agent Enterprise Fleet
Henry Mascot (
@henrymascot
) runs 3 machines with 14 specialized agents:
The setup:
  • 2 agents on GCP VM
  • 1 agent on Raspberry Pi at home
  • 1 node on Mac
The wild part: They SSH into each other and debug problems.
"This morning Ada was being slow. I asked Scotty 'what's wrong with Ada?' He SSH'd in, found the issue (bloated context), and fixed & restarted her."
Daily automations:
  • 7am briefing → Obsidian vault + ElevenLabs voice summary
  • Proactive Slack/email alerts
  • Fireflies transcripts → automatic action items
  • Performance tracking (emails, screen time, code written)
  • 40 custom skills built through conversation
Cost: ~$300/month AI + $50/month GCP + $120 Pi (one-time)
Output: Running a 60-person company across Africa & Middle East, building a stealth AI startup, staying present for family.
Link:
https://medium.com/@henrymascot/my-almost-agi-with-clawdbot
